detail: cause.refdes='R49' is neither source nor decoupling cap of rail 'VIN' (expected_dead_rails=['VIN'])

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. Components mentioned: F1, R49, J1. Rail / test point: Input rail (9–32 V nominal 24 V) measured at R49; fuse F1 in series before it.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no voltage reaching sense resistor R49
evidence.reasoning: Dump names R49 as the input sense resistor; matches bridge candidate R49.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no voltage reaching sense resistor R49
evidence.reasoning: An open/cold-jointed sense resistor breaks the input path.
R49 is in series with the VIN input path; an open R49 prevents VIN from reaching downstream regulators.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: R49 sense resistor opens the input rail; symptom is total no-power because VIN never reaches regulators.

detail: cause.refdes='U9' is neither source nor decoupling cap of rail '+1V2' (expected_dead_rails=['+1V2'])

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Components mentioned: U9 (TUSB8041), U10 (SN65DSI86), J24. Rail / test point: 1.2 V at C37.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally identifies U9 as the TUSB8041 USB hub; matches bridge candidate U9.
Internally shorted U9 collapses its 1.2V supply; modeled as shorted die.
Shorted U9 pulls down +1V2 rail (U9 is on +1V2).
U9 itself is dead; U10 also offline because shared 1.2V rail collapses.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U9 die-short shorts the 1.2V rail; both U9 (USB hub) and U10 (DSI bridge) go offline.

detail: cause.refdes='U10' is neither source nor decoupling cap of rail '+1V2' (expected_dead_rails=['+1V2'])

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Components mentioned: U9 (TUSB8041), U10 (SN65DSI86), J24. Rail / test point: 1.2 V at C37.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally identifies U10 as the SN65DSI86 DSI/eDP bridge; matches bridge candidate U10.
U10 die-short collapses its 1.2V supply; modeled as shorted.
Shorted U10 pulls down the shared +1V2 rail.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead
evidence.reasoning: U10 dead and U9 also offline due to shared rail collapse.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U10 die-short collapses 1.2V rail; both U9 and U10 go offline — display dark, USB dead.

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge, or Wi-Fi antenna routing interference into display path. Components mentioned: J24, U10 (SN65DSI86). reflow U10 if suspected cold joint.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally names U10 as the SN65DSI86 bridge whose cold joint may be the cause; matches bridge candidate.

evidence.source_quote_substring: reflow U10 if suspected cold joint
evidence.reasoning: Cold-joint failure is non-functional silicon; modeled as dead component, no rail cascade.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Cold-joint on U10 leaves DSI bridge non-functional; rails are intact, only U10 itself offline.

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: LPC UART responds and D11 is lit, but main CPU module does not start after sending `1P` power-on command; D11 goes out and nothing further happens. Likely cause: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged or a downstream rail is shorted, causing immediate shutdown; or the CPU module SODIMM slot has a bad contact. Components mentioned: U18 (LPC11U24), SODIMM slot.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally identifies U18 as the LPC11U24 System Controller; matches bridge candidate U18.

evidence.source_quote_substring: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged
evidence.reasoning: GPIO damage prevents U18 from sequencing rails — modeled as dead U18 (cannot enable downstream rails).
Without functional U18 issuing enables, the System Controller cannot bring up switched rails; symptom of D11 extinguishing reflects loss of switched 3.3V.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U18 GPIO damage means it cannot enable the downstream switched rails (3V3_PWR_AUX, USB_PWR); board doesn't power on past LPC stage.

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Rail / test point: LPC_VCC (3.3 V) at J22 pin 15; D11 illuminates when this rail is live.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally names D11 as the white indicator LED; matches bridge candidate D11.
Open LED — cosmetic, no rail cascade. Listed as anti-pattern: LED dark does not mean rail is dead.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: D11 LED itself open: indicator dark but LPC_VCC rail still live. Anti-pattern scenario where the symptom misleads diagnosis.
